reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless Chrome real-inference verification (fresh Download + cached auto-init passes) on the model page + wild correspondence page, desktop/mobile screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Real headless run (WASM, no-GPU): fresh profile → download-required + Download button → clicked → ready → 'Embed & compare' on the 6 samples produced a live 6×6 cosine matrix. Readout: 'backend WASM latency 1352 ms images 6 dims 384 registers 4'. Console errors: 0; failed network requests: 0. num_register_tokens=4 read live from the model config.
rubric.notes: onnx-community/dinov2-with-registers-small declares model_type 'dinov2_with_registers' and loads on the existing image-feature-extraction pipeline in transformers.js 3.7.5 — NO version pin needed. Inference in models/dinov2-registers/worker.js (Web Worker).

rubric.evidence: Expected shape confirmed live: raw last_hidden_state dims [1, 261, 384] = 1 [CLS] + 4 registers + 256 patch tokens (16×16 grid on 224px input). clsEmb is a 384-d L2-unit vector; patchSims/patchNorms are length-256; registerNorms length-4. Wild page: 256 patches/img, dense correspondence returned 256/256 confident matches for a cat↔cat pair at cosine≥0.5, far fewer for a genuine mismatch.
rubric.notes: The worker recovers numRegisters from config.num_register_tokens (fallback: tokens-1-largestSquare), so patch grid extraction is robust; patch tokens are sliced after CLS+registers.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance shows the exact source id onnx-community/dinov2-with-registers-small linked to its card (and the facebook/dinov2-with-registers-small origin), task image-feature-extraction, architecture ViT-S/14 + 4 register tokens, quantisation q8, backend WebAssembly, download ~25 MB, license Apache-2.0. Loader detail line confirms 'onnx-community/dinov2-with-registers-small · transformers.js · ~25 MB'.
rubric.notes: Honest disclosure of the register-token delta vs plain DINOv2 (Darcet et al. 2023) and a direct cross-link to the built dinov2-image-features demo for comparison — no invented API.

rubric.evidence: Two-pass headless test on a persistent profile. PASS 1 (fresh): loader state 'download-required' + Download (~25 MB) button — no silent transfer. PASS 2 (cached reload): loader auto-inits to 'ready', NO Download button present, page usable — auto-init from cache works per the shared createModelLoader contract.
rubric.notes: shared-auto-init-loader = pass; per-model 'Clear cached model' control present (visible in the wild-page screenshot).

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live announces checking→initialising→ready and embedding/done/error; a .err status surfaces failures ('Failed: …'). Verified download-required, ready, and 'Done.' states live across the model page and the wild page. The shared loader provides retry/recovery.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven this pass.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls verified live: drag/drop dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space handlers) + file picker, sample tray with per-image Remove buttons, Embed/Reset buttons, the see-inside heatmap-mode toggle (cosine-to-CLS / patch-norm, aria-pressed reflected), and an Inspect-image <select>. Wild page adds a match-confidence range that re-colours from cached patch embeddings without re-inferring. All keyboard-operable.
rubric.notes: A full driven touch traversal at 360px (dropzone, mode toggle, threshold slider tap targets) is the remaining check before flipping mobile to ok.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile shows the honest absent-model state and an empty matrix/see-inside ('Embed some images to inspect their features.'); the matrix, heatmaps, register-norm chips, and correspondence field appear ONLY after a real on-device run. All numbers (cosines, patch norms, register norms) are the model's real output.
rubric.notes: The patch-norm map is presented as the real per-token L2 norm — the registers 'artifact' story is shown from live data, not a canned illustration.

rubric.evidence: buttons-have-names and images-have-alt hold (sample thumbnails, preview, matrix thumbnails all carry alt). Heatmap/correspondence overlays use role=img with descriptive aria-label; the dropzone is role=button with keyboard handlers; the mode toggle uses aria-pressed; status region is role=status aria-live. Verified in headless DOM checks.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline; a full SR pass over the similarity table would be a nice add.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth<=innerWidth, zero clipped controls, console+network clean. Mobile screenshot confirms legible reflow of the heading, at-a-glance table (wrapped in .matrix-scroll), and controls with no horizontal overflow. The see-inside and correspondence layouts use auto-fit grids with minmax(min(280px,100%),1fr).
rubric.notes: Desktop + mobile kept needs-review pending a driven touch/keyboard matrix pass (automated signal only, per the harness rule).

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in models/dinov2-registers/worker.js (off the main thread); patch embeddings for correspondence are transferred (ArrayBuffer transfer list) rather than copied, and the wild-page confidence slider re-colours from the cached patch embeddings so dragging never re-inferences. Verified the page stays responsive during the ~25 MB load and the 6-image embed (1352 ms total).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP trace while dragging the correspondence slider on a large uploaded image would further confirm the paint path stays cheap.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (flex/min-inline-size:0), auto-fit grid tracks minmax(min(280px,100%),1fr), and wrap wide tables in an overflow-x:auto scroll container so long content never forces page overflow; let long unbreakable tokens wrap.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the see-inside and correspondence use .grid-2/.corr-pair auto-fit grids with minmax(min(...,100%),1fr); the At-a-glance table sits in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto); control rows use flex-wrap. Re-verified desktop+mobile scrollWidth<=innerWidth.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status and progress, keyboard-operable controls, image alternatives
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a role=status aria-live region; give every control an accessible name; expose canvas/grid visualisations via role=img + aria-label; make custom controls keyboard-operable and reflect toggle state with aria-pressed.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared createModelLoader status is role=status aria-live; heatmap/correspondence grids are role=img with aria-label; dropzone is role=button with Enter/Space; mode toggle reflects aria-pressed. buttons-have-names + images-have-alt assertions pass.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless DOM checks (buttons-have-names, images-have-alt); live-status-region assertion

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, run heavy work off the main thread, avoid recompute on slider tick
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy computation off the main thread and avoid recomputing on every input event.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — DINOv2-registers inference runs in a Web Worker; patch embeddings are transferred to the main thread and the correspondence slider re-colours from the cached embeddings rather than re-running inference.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: inference-off-main-thread source assertion; wild-page slider re-render path (no re-inference)

guidanceConsulted.query: catalogue to model page navigation transitions
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css (invariant 6).

openQuestions: Drive a full touch-interaction pass at 360×740 (dropzone, heatmap-mode toggle, correspondence threshold slider tap target) before flipping mobile support to ok.

A side-by-side patch-norm comparison against the plain dinov2-image-features demo on the SAME uploaded image would make the 'registers remove the artifacts' claim vivid — currently the contrast is explained + cross-linked, not shown in one view.
Dense correspondence uses brute-force nearest-patch (256×256 dot products); fine for one pair, but a KNN/approximate index would be needed to scale to many images or a video stream.

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for dinov2-registers: drive drop/upload + heatmap-mode toggle + correspondence slider on mobile with touch, verify tap targets/focus, then flip mobile support to ok.

Add a 'registers vs no-registers' side-by-side wild demo: run the SAME image through dinov2-registers and plain dinov2-small and show the two patch-norm maps together, so the artifact removal is visible in one view rather than across two pages.

Add a video/tracking multi-model demo: use the clean patch features to track a clicked region across frames (dense correspondence over time), showcasing why cleaner patch features matter for temporal tasks.

summary: dinov2-registers is a strong, honest demo of Meta's DINOv2-with-registers-small, distinct from the built plain-DINOv2 demo by the register-token story: same 384-d self-supervised descriptor, but the high-norm 'artifact' patches are removed for cleaner patch features. Real image-feature-extraction via onnx-community/dinov2-with-registers-small (loads on transformers.js 3.7.5 with no version pin) in a Web Worker, verified end-to-end in headless Chrome — fresh Download → live 6×6 similarity matrix (registers=4 confirmed), cached reload → auto-init no button, console clean both passes. The see-inside surface adds a patch-NORM heatmap (the artifacts story) and register-token norm chips beside the cosine-to-CLS map; the wild rung is a genuinely new dense patch-correspondence field (256/256 matches for a like pair, mostly grey for a mismatch); multi-model chains label-free retrieval into a ViT classifier. Desktop+mobile responsive-check automated-clean (kept needs-review pending a driven touch pass). Revision 1.
